recommendation.title: Single-Leg Call — Slight OTM, 1 Contract, Enter Post-Analyst Day
recommendation.entryNote: ⚠️ Analyst Day May 4 — IV elevated, enter AFTER to avoid crush. Stock has recovered +38% from the July 2024 outage lows. Falcon Flex added $774M deal value in Q1 (+31% seq) and Claude Opus 4.7 integration announced April 30. 12% upside to consensus is modest — thesis is on Falcon Flex acceleration and AI-powered security as 'must-have.' Note: high stock price means 1 contract = $3,800-4,600 premium.

companyOverview: Leading AI-native cybersecurity platform. Falcon Flex flexible deployment model adding $3.2B total deal value (+31% sequentially). Claude Opus 4.7 (Anthropic) integrated across Falcon platform. Project QuiltWorks ecosystem with Accenture, EY, IBM, OpenAI for AI-powered security. Q1 FY2026: ARR $4.44B (+22%), subscription gross margin 80%, FCF $279M (25% of revenue). 97% gross retention rate. Outage recovery essentially complete — stock +38% since lows.
catalysts: Falcon Flex driving large deal growth — $774M new in Q1, $3.2B total (+31% seq)
Claude Opus 4.7 integration across all Falcon modules (April 30)
Project QuiltWorks AI security ecosystem with major SI partners
97% gross retention — outage from July 2024 no longer impacting retention
Charlotte AI AgentWorks ecosystem for building secure AI agents
Analyst Day May 4 — potential product/partnership announcements
risks: Only 12% upside to consensus target — limited margin vs. risk
High stock price ($450): each contract requires $3,800–4,600 premium (1 contract only)
July 2024 outage litigation from Delta persisting — 'lingering cloud' on sentiment
IV elevated around May 4 analyst day — enter after to avoid crush
Competitive pressure from Microsoft Defender, SentinelOne, Palo Alto
AI capex slowdown would reduce demand for AI-integrated security tools
dilution: Standard executive and employee SBC dilution. No concerning capital raises. Strong FCF reduces need for equity financing.
